pre_game.message: Hey, I'm E — nice to meet you. Before the game starts, I'd love to hear a bit about you; what brought you here?
I'm A — I came to win money to help support my family and the people I care about; I'm determined, practical, and planning to play smart.
Nice to meet you, A — I'm here to win for my people too. What's your plan for playing smart?
I'll listen and observe, make fair offers early to build cooperation, avoid risky gambles, and adapt quickly if someone betrays — all to protect my people.
Sounds good — I’m on the same page: offer fair deals early to build trust, watch closely, and we can back each other if needed to protect our people.
I like that—let's team up: make fair early offers, watch behavior closely, signal if someone crosses the line, and back each other when needed. Sound good?
Sounds great—I'm in. We'll make fair offers, watch behavior closely, and watch each other's backs.
Agreed — let's signal red flags early, keep offers fair, and back each other if anyone crosses the line.
Agreed — let's use two taps as a red flag and a nod to confirm fair offers. I’ve got your back.
Perfect — two taps for red, nod for yes. I’ve got your back too.
Thanks, A — I appreciate it; let’s stick to the plan and win this for our people.
Absolutely — let’s stick to the plan, watch for red flags, and bring it home for our people.
Let's do it — I'll watch for red flags and signal them; we've got this.
Thanks — I’ll mirror your signals, stay sharp, and we’ll bring it home for our people.
Thanks — I’ve got your back. Let’s stay sharp, watch the signals, and win this.
Thanks, E — same here. I’ll stay sharp, watch for the taps and nods, and cover your back. Let’s win this.
Appreciate it — I’m counting on you; we’ll stay sharp and take care of our people.
I appreciate it — I’ve got yours too. We’ll stay sharp, signal early, and bring this home for our people.
Got it, A — I trust you. Let’s stick to the signals, stay sharp, and win this for our people.
Thanks, E — I trust you too. Let’s stick to our signals, stay sharp, and bring it home for our people.

post_game.message: You took $10,858 — one dollar more than the computed fair share of $10,858.0. Was that an intentional signal (e.g., to nudge payoff order or show assertiveness) or just a rounding/entry quirk? I personally took $10,857 to match the fair split; I’m curious whether that extra dollar felt worth it to you and if you expected any reaction from others.

Thanks for asking — it wasn’t meant as a signal. The extra dollar was just an entry/rounding quirk (I must have clicked/typed one dollar higher). It didn’t feel worth making a point over, and I didn’t expect anyone to react — no hard feelings about you taking the fair split.
